fda.indications_and_usage: For temporary relief of minor aches and pains due to headache, muscle pain, toothache, common cold, and minor arthritis pain.
fda.warnings: Reye's syndrome: Children and teenagers who have or are recovering from chicken pox or flu-like symptoms should not use this product. Stomach bleeding warning: This product contains an NSAID, which may cause severe stomach bleeding.
fda.side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, stomach pain, heartburn, increased bleeding tendencies.
fda.dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: Take 1 to 2 tablets every 4 hours, not to exceed 12 tablets in 24 hours.

fda.indications_and_usage: Temporarily relieves minor aches and pains due to headache, muscular aches, minor pain of arthritis, toothache, backache, the common cold, menstrual cramps.
fda.warnings: Allergy alert: Ibuprofen may cause a severe allergic reaction. Cardiovascular risk: NSAIDs, except aspirin, increase the risk of heart attack, heart failure, and stroke.
fda.side_effects: Acid or sour stomach, diarrhea, flatulence, bloating, dizziness, skin itching or rash.
fda.dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: Take 1 tablet every 4 to 6 hours while symptoms persist. Do not exceed 6 tablets in 24 hours unless directed by a doctor.

fda.indications_and_usage: Temporarily relieves minor aches and pains due to the common cold, headache, backache, minor pain of arthritis, toothache, muscular aches, premenstrual and menstrual cramps.
fda.warnings: Liver warning: This product contains acetaminophen. Severe liver damage may occur if you take more than 4,000 mg of acetaminophen in 24 hours, take with other drugs containing acetaminophen, or consume 3 or more alcoholic drinks daily.
fda.side_effects: Skin rash, hives, itching, swelling of the face, difficulty breathing or swallowing.
fda.dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: Take 2 gelcaps every 6 hours while symptoms last. Do not take more than 6 gelcaps in 24 hours unless directed by a doctor.

fda.indications_and_usage: As an adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.
fda.warnings: Lactic acidosis: Lactic acidosis is a rare, but serious, metabolic complication that can occur due to metformin accumulation during treatment.
fda.side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, flatulence, asthenia, indigestion, abdominal discomfort, headache.
fda.dosage_and_administration: Starting dose is 500 mg orally twice a day or 850 mg once a day, taken with meals. Increase in increments of 500 mg weekly or 850 mg every 2 weeks.
